summary: Body copy ≥16px, line height ≥150% (higher for light-on-dark), use type scale for hierarchy.
Ensure minimum contrast ratios for body and large text; never use color alone for meaning; verify all text legibility for accessibility.
Form labels always visible; no placeholder-as-label or disappearing labels; click/tap targets must be at least 48px.
Use quick, non-disruptive transitions; limit motion; offer pause controls; ensure responsive design for phone, tablet, and desktop.
[GENERAL email] Animated GIFs must have key message in frame 1 and no autoplay motion over ~5s without static fallback for Outlook accessibility.
All images require descriptive alt text; videos and audio must have transcripts available for accessibility.
Accessibility standards must comply with WCAG guidelines from wcag.com.
Confirm patient (DTC) audience in briefs before writing; language rules differ by audience in patient materials.
Use inclusive person-first phrasing like "someone/people with dermatomyositis" in patient (DTC) materials; use "patients" only in HCP materials.
The patient brochure is the master reference for visuals, messaging, and tone across all LISRAYA materials.
Each section is a 600px wide, table-based block with 32px top/bottom padding, declared background color, and no external margins for direct stacking.
Section metadata requires background color, DM status, assigned footnote symbols (†, ‡) to prevent collisions, and CTA presence declared at assembly.
Footnote symbols superscript, assigned in appearance order at assembly; sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) for multiple claims, not hard-coded daggers.
Locked header, footer, ISI inserted by core team; adjacent sections cannot have decorative elements visually dependent on those locked components (e.g., waves continuing into footer).
Dark-background sections cannot be adjacent; assembler must ensure separation using declared backgrounds.
Use HEX colors, px sizing, inline CSS only, and duplicate bgcolor attributes for Outlook in email code.

Lisraya palette tiers: PRIMARY (Brand Blue #00529b, Sunshine #faa31b, Gold #ffc60a), SECONDARY (Sky Blue #358CCB, Deep Blue #003D74, Dark Navy #011E45), TERTIARY (Coral, Light Blue, Golden Sand, Graphite, White); use hierarchy primary > secondary > tertiary.
Use palette in primary > secondary > tertiary ratio; blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors only as accents and surfaces.
Headlines use Brand Blue or White (dark backgrounds); body text Graphite #212121 (light) or White (dark, higher line height); links Brand Blue #00529b or White, underlined.
In charts, use Brand Blue + Sky Blue for main series, Sunshine/Gold only for key figure emphasis, gray for placebo.
Sunshine/Gold color is exclusively for highlighting key chart figures, prohibited for other series.
Callout surfaces: Light Blue 70% tint for data callouts, Golden Sand 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.
Use coral sparingly as a tertiary accent; never for primary CTAs, headlines, data, or email sections unless specified by the brief.
Maintain logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C (#ffc60a) in all uses, including logo and pattern/graphic marks.
Only full palette colors or specified tints allowed: campaign headline 'D'/'M' at ~40% tint (Gold/Brand Blue), Light Blue callout 70%, Golden Sand callout 30%, wave top 70% transparency.
Use only approved fixed-stop gradients: Brand Blue→Dark Navy, Sky Blue→Deep Blue, Sunshine→75% Gold, Golden Sand→White→Light Blue, Golden Sand→75% Gold→Sunshine, plus Masthead gradient.
In masthead/banner, horizontal gradients run dark to light left→right; use Sunshine→light gold (#ffe05c) behind LISRAYA logo at lightest end for contrast.
Test all text/background contrast via webaim or equivalent; minimum WCAG AA ratios: 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 for ≥24px or 19px bold large text.
Use Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy; no Gold/Sunshine text on White.
Avoid using color alone for meaning; combine with icons, depth, clear text, or visual feedback for accessibility.

Gradient Callout CTA: full-width 600px banner, orange/yellow gradient background, headline top-third Brand Blue #00529b Nunito Sans 800 28px center, button below Deep Blue #003D74 fill white 18px Nunito Sans 800 with gold #FFC20E arrow, sized 248×64px, padding 48px 25px 56px 25px.
Use gradient_callout.png as section background, cover, no-repeat, centered, 600px wide; fallback solid Sunshine #faa31b; no recolor or re-host.
Rule (cta): Background image skipped only in rare cases; remove background-url/size/repeat/position only, keep Sunshine #faa31b fill, headline/button color, and button radius unchanged.
CTA buttons must use asymmetric border-radius 49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px exactly; never simplify, swap corners, or round decimals, even without background.

Icon Callout Box: Light Blue #E6F0F9 box, Brand Blue #00529b Nunito Sans 800 ~21px/28px left-aligned headline, white rounded-square badge with blue stroke icon half off left edge, left padding ≥ half badge width + 24px, on white background.
Geometry: badge width W (default 80px), inset blue box left by W/2, badge center on box left edge; vertical center text and badge with top 50% + margin-top -H/2; box left-padding = W/2 + 24px.
Iconography corners always have border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL), white badge uses scaled 20px 4px 20px 4px; never uniform, swapped, or flipped.
White icon badge always has box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18) for subtle lift off blue box, preventing flat appearance.

Use absolute 50/50 badge overhang; Outlook ignores it—pre-composite badge PNG or bake into background; else accept Outlook badge flush left; keep Light Blue fill, 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow.
Email body width fixed at 600px centered; sections edge-to-edge 600px; mobile breakpoint 480px collapses multi-columns; full-bleed backgrounds 600px, text inset 24px sides.
Internal section grid follows 12-column model; layouts: 1-col, 2-col equal (50/50), or image/text split approx 40/60.

All sections have 24px side padding; vertical spacing uses 8px units; each section has 32px top/bottom padding; no external margins; Heading→body 16px; paragraph→paragraph 16px; body→callout/CTA 24px; image→caption 8px.
Primary CTA: #00529b bg, #ffffff text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, padding 14x32px, min 48px height, 24px rounded pill; Secondary: white bg, 2px #00529b border, #00529b text.
One primary CTA allowed per section maximum.
Wave forms use Sunshine/Gold or Sky Blue/Deep Blue colorways with long/short variants; top wave layer at 70% opacity overlays base (Gold over Sunshine, Blue over Deep Blue).
Use waves alone or over photos/gradients to enhance layout, frame content, or add depth; only approved colors allowed.
Masthead uses Sunshine to light gold gradient (0C,10M,78Y,0K), with LISRAYA logo mark at lightest end (ast_lisraya_masthead_logo_mark) for copy.
Photography must feature warm, realistic patient lifestyle moments (walking dog, beach, BBQ), showing return to everyday life and 'getting back to themselves' (imagery.treatment).
Images must use Accent shape (§2.5) asymmetric rounded-corner crops, not plain rectangles or circles (image.crop.treatment).
Waves and gradients can overlay photography in imagery.
Every image must include descriptive alt text to meet brand accessibility standards.

Core container: rectangle with one diagonal large radius (5:1 ratio) and small 6px radius opposite corners; print sizes 1.25"/0.0625", 0.5"/0.0625", 0.4375"/0.0625"; email @96dpi sizes 120px/6px, 48px/6px, 42px/6px.
Use consistent diagonal orientation of accent shape in all email sections; default radius: large top-left & bottom-right, 6px on top-right & bottom-left corners.
Charts and tables use uniform 12px rounded corners on all corners, not the asymmetric brand accent shape.
Charts/infographics: clean, scannable, consistent; container with uniform rounded corners, Light Blue (#E6F0F9) panel fill, Brand Blue rounded header bar.
Use Brand Blue #00529b and Sky Blue #358CCB as dominant chart colors; Sunshine #faa31b and Gold #ffc60a for key data emphasis; gray for placebo/comparator.
Bars and chart icons have rounded tops for approachability per chart.bar.style in imagery rules.
Chart headlines use Agenda (print)/Nunito Sans Bold+ (email), ALL CAPS, Brand Blue, thin Gold rule beneath (chart.header.font, case, color, rule_color).
Statistical callouts: large bold % figure as hero, supporting copy lighter weight below (chart.stat_callout.weight).
Footnotes in charts use footnote.type_scale, noticeably smaller and clearly separated from primary data.
Icons use minimal single-color line illustrations with consistent stroke weight, rounded caps, Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds; new icons must follow this style.
Icon colors and backgrounds must use only brand palette colors for adjustments.
Ensure 32×32px minimum icon size and adequate scale/contrast for low-vision users in email (icon.min_render) under GENERAL accessibility rules.
Use generous white space, clean layouts, max one focal point per section with contrast; break text walls with imagery/icons ('Show, don't tell').
Alternate My Compass section backgrounds using White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, or gradients; declare background color in metadata to avoid adjacent identical blocks.
Use bold headlines, simple benefit statements, and a patient quote as the canonical content block, following the patient brochure template for all LISRAYA materials.
Always write LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all materials; supersedes Feb 2026 guide's title-case; bind brand_name.case to all-caps.
Include ® only on first prominent mention with generic name lowercase in parentheses (brepocitinib) in external docs; do not repeat ® thereafter.
Parent company is Priovant Therapeutics, Inc.; sign-off located only in locked footer, never added within sections.
Typography: Print headlines use Agenda; digital/email headlines and all body copy use Nunito Sans (bolder weights, Title Case); Word/PPT/Excel fallback uses Aptos; footers match body family.
Email typography font stack: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; load Nunito Sans via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported, fallback for Outlook/clients.

Typography email type scale: H1 28px/36px Nunito Sans ExtraBold/Bold; H2 22px/30px Bold; H3 18px/26px Bold/SemiBold; Body 16px/24px Regular; Secondary 14px/21px Regular sparingly; Footnote 12px/18px smaller, separated.
Body copy minimum 16px; secondary body 14px sparingly, never primary; running text never below 12px; ISI sizing locked in component.
Body copy line height minimum 150%; increase to 160% for light text on dark backgrounds (e.g., 16px/26px), applied via body.line_height token.
Use clear typography hierarchy with distinct sizes/weights; never place adjacent text blocks at identical size and weight to differentiate hierarchy levels.
Headlines/headings use title case: capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions.
Campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' must always be set in ALL CAPS.
Chart/infographic headlines use ALL CAPS in Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback) typography.
Body copy is sentence case; no ALL CAPS for body, buttons, subheads except campaign and chart/infographic headlines.
Bold only headings, one key phrase/claim per paragraph, statistical callout figures, callout text (§1.7), first brand name in benefits; avoid excessive (15%) or filler bolding.
copy_editorial: Avoid using bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on one string, except in chart headlines and campaign headline lockups; max two emphasis types per element.
No underlining except hyperlinks; italics only for study names, notes ('Note:'), and citations, not for emphasis in patient copy.
Use 'dermatomyositis (DM)' at first mention in each standalone section; use 'DM' thereafter in running copy, but always spell out 'dermatomyositis' in headlines, headings, subheads.
Avoid abbreviation 'DM' in headlines, headings, subheads; always use full word 'dermatomyositis' for clarity, accessibility, and SEO.
In patient materials, use "someone/people with dermatomyositis" (not "patients"); use "care team" instead of "staff"; "patients" allowed only in HCP materials.
Spell out numbers 0–9; use numerals for 10+; always use numerals and % sign for percentages, ages, ratios.
Use empathetic, supportive tone without fear-based language when citing clinical data; avoid oversimplification (e.g., VALOR study LISRAYA 50% symptom improvement).
Use Oxford/serial comma in lists of three or more items.
YOU DESERVE MORE headline uses all caps Agenda font (email fallback Nunito Sans ExtraBold), Gold/Sunshine or Blue color with 'D' and 'M' at 40% tint, stacked three-line left-aligned in hero.
Full campaign lockup copy must read exactly: 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.'
Use three callout styles (Main/key-point, Secondary/data, Text-heavy); never let two identical styles compete on one screen or section.
Main callout: bold Brand Blue text, centered, with 2px Gold horizontal rules above/below, 16px padding, rule width 60–100% of text block.
Secondary/data callout: Light Blue box 70% tint, bold Brand Blue text, optional 1-line icon, 20px padding, corner radius as per accent-shape §2.5
Text-heavy callout uses 30% Golden Sand box, bold Brand Blue headline, line icons, bold Sky Blue subheads, regular Graphite body, 24px padding, 2-column collapses mobile.
Use the exact campaign headline lockup: "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
copy_editorial: Product benefits must be verbatim with intro "LISRAYA..." plus bullets: Increase muscle strength, Improve skin symptoms, Rely less on steroids, Do more activities you love.

Use verbatim mechanism-of-action text: primary "Unlike other treatments for dermatomyositis (DM), which suppress immune system generally, LISRAYA targets root cause." Alternate: "LISRAYA works by turning off specific inflammatory switches stuck 'on'.
Use the exact administration statement: "LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.
Doctor-discussion CTA must exactly read: "Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.
Name patient support "My Compass Support" with Personalized Communication via PAL (text, phone, email, in person) and Authorization Support.
LISRAYA voice is confident (clear first-of-its-kind), warm (genuine empathy for DM patients), and clear (simple, accurate language), tone flexes but core constant.
Clinical claims must include † footnote markers with distinct, smaller footnote text per type scale, separated per §1.2.
Patient quotes must be attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, styled per patient_story.quote.style, and paired with warm lifestyle imagery.
Priovant logo, copyright, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, ISI in locked header/footer only; never in content sections.
Use brand colors in hierarchy: #00529B Deep Blue dominant, #FAA31B Amber Orange secondary accent, #FFC60A Golden Yellow tertiary accent.
Agenda is the primary headline font for all headlines/headers; Nunito Sans is used for all print body copy; email uses semantic surface variants with stack fallbacks.
Use signature CTA SVG (viewBox 0 0 180 58.5, rendered 180x59) filled with gradient #FFDF55 to #FAA21B for CTAs.
